title: halimium viscosum
intro: Halimium viscosum, also known as sticky rockrose, is a perennial plant species within the Cistaceae family, characterized by its sticky or viscid stems and leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Halimium viscosum are attributed to its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and aid in the healing process. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of infusions or salves, are used to treat minor cuts and scrapes, as well as to promote wound healing and skin health.
